title: Query system: pattern compiler and cursor
description:
Implement the S-expression query system for structural pattern matching on parse trees.

Deliverables:
- query.go: Query compiler — recursive descent parser over S-expression pattern strings. Produces:
  - []queryStep: compiled pattern nodes (symbol, field, captureIDs [4]uint32, alternativeIndex, flags: isNamed, isImmediate, isLastChild, isPassThrough, isDeadEnd, rootPatternGuaranteed)
  - []queryPattern: per-pattern metadata (offset into steps, predicate info)
  - []PredicateStep: predicate data stored separately
  - patternMap: sorted by symbol for binary search lookup
- QueryCursor: walks tree using TreeCursor, maintains active QueryState entries. Matching algorithm: descend (create states for matching patterns), advance (check nodes against next step), ascend (yield completed matches). State deduplication via primary_state_ids.
- captureListPool: slice-based pool for in-progress capture lists.
- Pattern features: named nodes, anonymous nodes, wildcards (_), fields, captures (@name), alternations ([...]), quantifiers (+, *, ?), anchors (.), negated fields (!field).
- Predicate step extraction (PredicatesForPattern) — not evaluation.
- Standard predicate helpers: #eq?, #match?, #any-of?

Tests:
- Query patterns from real tree-sitter highlight queries (Go, JavaScript)
- All pattern features: wildcards, fields, captures, alternations, quantifiers, anchors, negated fields
- Predicate extraction correctness
- QueryCursor range restriction (startByte/endByte)
- Compare query results with C tree-sitter on same input + patterns
- Edge cases: empty patterns, deeply nested patterns, patterns with many captures

Reference: docs/design.md §7, C source: query.c
